acceptance_targets: [CH11-BKR-001] PASS if the revised ending (anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:367-371) lets a reader name who received C-11 (or clearly defined role labels) and one immediate consequence set in motion before the final line; FAIL if the send remains anonymous and consequence-free.

[CH11-CRF-001] PASS if snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:281-283 contains direct quoted profanity (not paraphrased swearing) tied to a clear target and followed by an observable response; FAIL if swearing remains euphemized or offscreen.

[CH11-CRF-002] PASS if the revised meeting span names at least one specific prior consequence (victim/group + concrete harm) and directly links it to Ina's delay request; FAIL if stakes remain only abstract terms like 'history,' 'windows,' or 'bodies.'

[CH11-DLG-001] PASS if the revised council span shows at least two interruption/overlap beats and at least three speakers with clearly distinct rhythm/word-choice patterns; FAIL if turns remain uniformly polished and interchangeable.

[CH11-DLG-002] PASS if revised versions of the cited lines default to contractions for Ina/Minjae (or include explicit emphasis cues justifying full forms); FAIL if uncontracted phrasing remains casual baseline speech.

[CH11-PRO-001] PASS if the revised line-273 memory sequence contains no more than one sentence beginning with 'She thought of' and each of the three memory beats adds distinct concrete detail; FAIL if the triple anaphora remains unchanged.

[CH11-PRO-002] PASS if revised versions of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:3, :55, :141, :329, and :369 contain at most two 'like' simile constructions total while preserving concrete sensory detail; FAIL if most of those lines still rely on the same simile template.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised ending (anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:367-371) lets a reader name who received C-11 (or clearly defined role labels) and one immediate consequence set in motion before the final line; FAIL if the send remains anonymous and consequence-free.
findings.problem: The chapter lands the required access-loss beat but withholds a concrete counter-move outcome: forwarding memo C-11 to unnamed off-book addresses keeps the ending atmospheric rather than causally propulsive.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the closing turn at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:367 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:371 so Ina's off-book send identifies recipient role(s) and immediate operational risk (e.g., leak exposure, retaliation trigger, or next-action deadline), while preserving that she remains locked out at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:361.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:281-283 contains direct quoted profanity (not paraphrased swearing) tied to a clear target and followed by an observable response; FAIL if swearing remains euphemized or offscreen.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift at peak conflict: the narration uses the euphemistic placeholder 'swore under his breath' instead of rendering the actual verbal violence. This defuses scene pressure where the text should risk specificity.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:281 with explicit spoken profanity and a concrete target (person/object/situation), plus a reaction beat that changes leverage in the exchange.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised meeting span names at least one specific prior consequence (victim/group + concrete harm) and directly links it to Ina's delay request; FAIL if stakes remain only abstract terms like 'history,' 'windows,' or 'bodies.'
findings.problem: The meeting argues life-and-death stakes largely through policy abstraction ('institutional hesitation,' 'status quo pays in bodies') rather than embodied prior consequence, which reads as timidity at the exact point causal cost should be concrete.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:89-101 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:133-135, add one concrete prior harm datum (who was hit, what happened physically/economically, and how quickly) that Ina uses as tactical evidence for delay.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised council span shows at least two interruption/overlap beats and at least three speakers with clearly distinct rhythm/word-choice patterns; FAIL if turns remain uniformly polished and interchangeable.
findings.problem: Council-room dialogue trends toward polished transcript cadence: turns are orderly and syntactically similar under high pressure, muting idiolect separation and leverage volatility.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the exchange centered on snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:67-92 to introduce interruptions, clipped rebuttals, and clearer per-character speech fingerprints (Song analytic hedging, Bae colder operational cynicism, Kwon inevitability frame, Ina corrective bursts).

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised versions of the cited lines default to contractions for Ina/Minjae (or include explicit emphasis cues justifying full forms); FAIL if uncontracted phrasing remains casual baseline speech.
findings.problem: High-contraction voices intermittently drift into stiff full forms ('I will call...' / 'This is not...') without strong emphasis markers, weakening spoken naturalism in pressured scenes.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:25 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:281 to contraction-forward phrasing unless full-form diction is explicitly motivated by emphasis or role-play in the beat.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised line-273 memory sequence contains no more than one sentence beginning with 'She thought of' and each of the three memory beats adds distinct concrete detail; FAIL if the triple anaphora remains unchanged.
findings.problem: A repeated sentence construction appears three times in one beat ('She thought of ...'), creating formulaic interiority rather than escalating cognitive texture.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the memory cascade at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:273 so only one clause uses the 'She thought of' lead-in; vary the other two into distinct syntactic forms with sharper sensory/causal detail.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised versions of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:3, :55, :141, :329, and :369 contain at most two 'like' simile constructions total while preserving concrete sensory detail; FAIL if most of those lines still rely on the same simile template.
findings.problem: Metaphor template repetition: frequent '[thing] like [thing]' similes at major beats becomes a default prose engine, flattening texture through predictable construction reuse.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the cited lines, keep no more than two similes total and replace remaining comparisons with direct sensory/action description specific to place, body, or motion.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when stakes demand it: show coercion mechanics, sexual negotiation, grief rituals, and financial desperation at scene level. In edge cases, prefer precision over decorum; protect character dignity through perspective accuracy, not by omitting the hard material.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene requires bodily, economic, or moral consequence and the draft retreats into abstract phrasing, rewrite with concrete action, sensation, and decision impact before proceeding.
aesthetic_risk_policy.operational_resolution_rule: When uncertain between tasteful distance and narrative truth, choose the version that preserves causal accountability and embodied consequence, then tune language for clarity rather than softness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, legal language, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter structures deliberately: high-density dialogue vs interior reflection, multi-scene public collision vs two-scene intimate pressure, aftermath inventory vs anticipatory dread. Never let two consecutive chapters share the same pacing architecture or turn mechanism.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsentimental literary diction that permits technical vocabulary, profanity, and class-marked speech without flattening idiolects.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person centered on Seo Ina, with brief controlled widenings in public-event chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternating pressure waves: compressed clauses in operational stress, longer breath-based lines in grief and reflection.
